title: Bandhua Mukti Morcha v. Union of India

parties.petitioner: Bandhua Mukti Morcha (Bonded Labour Liberation Front)

legal_principles: Right to live with human dignity
summary: Bandhua Mukti Morcha wrote letters to the Supreme Court about bonded labourers in Haryana quarries. The Court treated the letters as writ petitions (epistolary jurisdiction) and ordered release of bonded labourers. Held that right to live with dignity is a part of right to life under Article 21 and bonded labour violates this right.
key_holdings: Bonded labour is unconstitutional under Article 23
Right to live with human dignity is part of Article 21
Court's epistolary jurisdiction allows letters to be treated as PILs
State must rehabilitate released bonded labourers
Minimum wage violation amounts to forced labour under Article 23
key_quotes: Article 21 includes the right to live with basic human dignity.
Letters addressed to the court can be treated as writ petitions.
why_important: Expanded the scope of PIL by allowing letters to be treated as petitions. Linked minimum wage compliance to Article 23's prohibition on forced labour.
relevance_to_legal_finance_system: Minimum wage compliance is a legal obligation with tax and financial implications for employers.
